meaning: adv.浠ヨ船鎶戠殑鍙ｅ惢,浠ヨ交瑙嗙殑鎬佸害
examples: He spoke disparagingly of his colleagues.
No one spoke disparagingly of her father in her hearing.
Speaking disparagingly of people in front of their friends.
So whenever my editorial friends speak disparagingly of Rupert, I usually say nothing.
Many scientists who write disparagingly about religion focus on the conscious, explicit beliefs in God and the supernatural.
